properties.name: Advice
properties.location: Acheron, Buxton, Granton, Marysville, Narbethong, St Fillans, Taggerty, Wilks Creek, Cambarville, East Warburton, Fernshaw, Limestone, Mcmahons Creek, Murrindindi, Rubicon, Thornton, Warburton, Whanregarwen
properties.webBody:
This Flood Watch is being issued for the Goulburn River at Acheron.

Minor flooding possible at Seymour Tuesday evening.

Moderate rainfall since Monday has caused river and creek level rises in the Goulburn River catchment, where minor flooding is possible at Seymour from Tuesday evening.

Widespread rainfall is forecast to continue through to Friday, which is likely to result in further rises and areas of flooding across the Goulburn River catchment.

A Flood Watch is current for Parts of North East and Central Victoria.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do:
  • Decide what you and your family will do if flooding impacts you. For information on how to prepare go to www.ses.vic.gov.au/get-ready/floodsafe.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
Impacts in your area: 
  • Areas around rivers and streams may be flooded.
  • Heavy rain over fire affected areas can increase the risk of landslides. 
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 01/07/2026 05:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the Goulburn River Downstream to Hughes Creek

Moderate rainfall since Monday has caused river and creek level rises in the Goulburn River catchment,where minor flooding is occurring at Seymour. 

A minor flood peak is expected at Seymour overnight Tuesdayinto Wednesday.

Widespread rainfall is forecast to continue through to Friday, which is likely to result in further and renewedrises and areas of flooding across the Goulburn River catchment.

A Flood Watch is current for Parts of North East and Central Victoria.

Hughes Creek:

Minor flooding is occurring along Hughes Creek at Tarcombe Rd.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  •  Floodwater is toxic - never play or swim in floodwater.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
  •  Caravan parks and camping grounds may be flooded.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 01/07/2026 11:00 am or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.location: the Seven Creeks downstream of Euroa
properties.webBody:
This Flood Watch is being issued for the Seven Creeks downstream of Euroa.

A series of cold fronts are forecast to cross Victoria through to Friday, which is likely to bring widespread moderate rainfall across north east and central Victoria from Tuesday.
This rainfall is likely to result in minor to moderate flooding from Tuesday across the Flood Watch area, with isolated major flooding possible. 

Minor flooding is occuring on the Seven Creeks at Strathbogie and may occur at Euroa Wednesday morning.

Catchments in the Flood Watch area are relatively wet.

The situation is being closely monitored, and Flood Warnings will be issued as required.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do:
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Download your Local Flood Guide to understand local flood impacts www.ses.vic.gov.au/plan-and-stay-safe/flood-guides
  • Decide what you and your family will do if flooding impacts you. For information on how to prepare go to www.ses.vic.gov.au/get-ready/floodsafe.
Impacts in your area: 
  • Areas around rivers and streams may be flooded.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 01/07/2026 03:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.location: the Goulburn River Lake Eildon to Seymour
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the Goulburn River Lake Eildon to Seymour

Minor flooding occuring at Seymour.

Moderate rainfall since Monday has caused river and creek level rises in the Goulburn River catchment,where minor flooding is occurring at Seymour. 

A minor flood peak is expected at Seymour overnight Tuesday into Wednesday.

Widespread rainfall is forecast to continue through to Friday, which is likely to result in further and renewedrises and areas of flooding across the Goulburn River catchment.

A Flood Watch is current for Parts of North East and Central Victoria

Goulburn River Lake Eildon to Seymour:

Minor flooding is occurring along the Goulburn River Lake Eildon to Seymour. 

The Goulburn River at Seymour is currently at 4.39 m and rising, with minor flooding. 

The Goulburn River at Seymour may peak near 4.90 m overnight Tuesday into Wednesday, with minor flooding. 

The river mayremain above the minor flood level (3.80 m) until Wednesday afternoon.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Download your Local Flood Guide to understand local flood impacts www.ses.vic.gov.au/plan-and-stay-safe/flood-guides
  • Decide what you and your family will do if flooding impacts you. 
  • For information on how to prepare go to www.ses.vic.gov.au/get-ready/floodsafe
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
Impacts in your area: 
  • Areas around rivers and streams may be flooded.
  • Heavy rain over fire affected areas can increase the risk of landslides. 
  • Local roads may be closed and low bridges may be underwater. 
  • Caravan parks and camping grounds may be flooded.
VICSES Seymour Unit advises of the following local road closures:
  • Tallarook - Pyalong Road, Tallarook between Sharps Rd, Taylors Rd and Gerard Rd
  • Delatite Road, Seymour
  • White Gate Road, Seymour - South of Seymour-Pyalong Rd.
  • Ennis Road, Tallarook - Hume Freeway end
  • Ashes Bridge Road, Tallarook
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 01/07/2026 11:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.webBody:
This Flood Watch is being issued for the Broken Creek.

Minor to moderate flooding likely in the flood watch area from Tuesday, with isolated major flooding possible.

A series of cold fronts are forecast to cross Victoria through to Friday, which is likely to bring widespread moderate rainfall across north east and central Victoria From Tuesday.

This rainfall is likely to result in minor to moderate flooding from Tuesday across the Flood Watch area, with isolated major flooding possible. Renewed flooding is possible in the Kiewa River catchment, where a Flood Warning remains current.

Catchments in the Flood Watch area are relatively wet.

The situation is being closely monitored, and Flood Warnings will be issued as required.

Catchments likely to be affected include:
  • Upper Murray and Mitta Mitta Rivers
  • Ovens and King Rivers
  • Broken River
  • Broken Creek
  • Seven and Castle Creeks
  • Goulburn River Eildon to Seymour
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do:
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Download your Local Flood Guide to understand local flood impacts www.ses.vic.gov.au/plan-and-stay-safe/flood-guides
  • Decide what you and your family will do if flooding impacts you. For information on how to prepare go to www.ses.vic.gov.au/get-ready/floodsafe.
Impacts in your area: 
  • Areas around rivers and streams may become flooded.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 01/07/2026 02:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:

  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the King River at Docker. 

Minor flooding is possible at Docker Road Bridge overnight Tuesday into Wednesday. 

Moderate rainfall across the Ovens and King River catchments since Monday has caused significant river level rises along the upper King River, with further rain forecast through to Friday. 

Minor flooding is possible at Docker Road Bridge from overnight Tuesday into Wednesday. This situation is being closely monitored, and Flood Warnings will be updated as required.

A Flood Watch is current for other parts of North East and Central Victoria.

King River
The King River at Docker Road Bridge is currently at 1.82 metres and rising and may exceed the minor flood level of 3.70 metres overnight Tuesday into Wednesday.
The river may remain above the minor flood level of 3.70 m metres for the next few days, and further rises are possible with forecast rain. 
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground ahead of further rainfall from Tuesday through to Friday. 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 01/07/2026 01:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.location: the Goulburn River from Goulburn Weir to Shepparton
properties.webBody:
This Flood Advice is being issued for campers along the edge of the Goulburn River from Goulburn Weir to Shepparton.

Goulburn Murray Water is releasing water from the Goulburn Weir, which may result in the river rising quickly overnight within the river channel and below the minor flood level. 

Campers and visitors close to the river should relocate to higher banks away from the river's edge and closely monitor conditions overnight. 

Riverine flooding of roads, towns and homes is not expected during this warning period. The situation is being monitored closely and further warnings will be issued if conditions change. 
Stay informed - monitor local conditions and remain alert to the river rising quickly overnight. 
What you should do
  • If you are camped within the Goulburn River channel, move your camp away from the River's edge and remain alert to the river rising quickly overnight. 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes. 
  • Monitor weather forecasts and river levels. Go to www.bom.gov.au/vic/warnings/ 
Impacts in your area
  • Low-lying campgrounds on the Goulburn River may become inundated or isolated if the river rises quickly overnight. 
This message was issued by State Emergency Service 

The next update is expected by 01/07/2026 10:00 am or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au 
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app 
  • Facebook or Twitter (Vicfloods) 
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.   
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.
properties.text:
ADVICE - RIVERINE FLOOD - Stay Informed
Incident Location: the Goulburn River from Goulburn Weir to Shepparton
Issue Date: Tuesday 30 June 2026, 06:53 pm
Next Update: Wednesday 01 July 2026, 10:00 am

More details at http://emergency.vic.gov.au/respond/#!/warning/42069/moreinfo

This Flood Advice is being issued for campers along the edge of the Goulburn River from Goulburn Weir to Shepparton.

Goulburn Murray Water is releasing water from the Goulburn Weir, which may result in the river rising quickly overnight within the river channel and below the minor flood level. 

Campers and visitors close to the river should relocate to higher banks away from the river's edge and closely monitor conditions overnight. 

Riverine flooding of roads, towns and homes is not expected during this warning period. The situation is being monitored closely and further warnings will be issued if conditions change. 

Stay informed - monitor local conditions and remain alert to the river rising quickly overnight.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This Flood Watch is being issued for the Broken River.

Minor to moderate flooding likely in the flood watch area from Tuesday, with isolated major flooding possible.

A series of cold fronts are forecast to cross Victoria through to Friday, which is likely to bring widespread moderate rainfall across north east and central Victoria from Tuesday.

This rainfall is likely to result in minor to moderate flooding from Tuesday across the Flood Watch area, with isolated major flooding possible. Renewed flooding is possible in the Kiewa River catchment, where a Flood Warning remains current.

Catchments in the Flood Watch area are relatively wet.

The situation is being closely monitored, and Flood Warnings will be issued as required.

Catchments likely to be affected include:
  • Upper Murray and Mitta Mitta Rivers
  • Ovens and King Rivers
  • Broken River
  • Broken Creek
  • Seven and Castle Creeks
  • Goulburn River Eildon to Seymour
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do:
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Download your Local Flood Guide to understand local flood impacts www.ses.vic.gov.au/plan-and-stay-safe/flood-guides
  • Decide what you and your family will do if flooding impacts you. For information on how to prepare go to www.ses.vic.gov.au/get-ready/floodsafe.
Impacts in your area: 
  • Areas around rivers and streams may be flooded.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 01/07/2026 03:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:

properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the Kiewa River to Mongans Bridge.

Minor flooding possible from Tuesday morning.

Renewed minor minor flooding likely at Kiewa mainstream and Bandiana from Tuesday evening.

Moderate to heavy rainfall is forecast for the remainder of Tuesday and is expected to continue through to Friday, which is likely to result in renewed river level rises across the Kiewa River catchment.

Renewed minor flooding is likely at Bandiana from Tuesday evening and along the Kiewa River main stream from Wednesday morning. A Flood Watch is current for Parts of North East Victoria.

Kiewa River to Mongans Bridge:
River level rises and minor flooding is possible at Mongans Bridge from Tuesday evening.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Contact family members and neighbours to ensure they are aware of the situation.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 01/07/2026 12:00 pm or as the situation changes.

This Flood Watch is being issued for the Goulburn River at King Parrot Creek.

Minor flooding possible at Seymour Tuesday evening

Moderate rainfall since Monday has caused river and creek level rises in the Goulburn River catchment,where minor flooding is possible at Seymour from Tuesday evening.

Widespread rainfall is forecast to continue through to Friday, which is likely to result in further rises and areasof flooding across the Goulburn River catchment.

A Flood Watch is current for Parts of North East and Central Victoria.

King Parrot Creek:

River level rises are occurring along the King Parrot Creek.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is for the Seven Creeks to Euroa and replaces the Flood Watch issued earlier on Tuesday. 

Minor Flooding is likely at Euroa overnight Tuesday into Wednesday. 

Moderate rainfall has been observed across the Seven and Castle Creek catchments since Monday evening.

This has resulted in rises in creek levels, with minor flooding occurring at Strathbogie and Galls Gap Road.

Minor flooding is likely at Euroa from overnight Tuesday into Wednesday.Further moderate to heavy rainfall is forecast to continue until Friday across the Seven and Castle Creek catchments.

A Flood Watch is current for Parts of North East Victoria.

Seven Creeks to Euroa

Minor flooding is occurring along the Seven Creeks at Strathbogie and Galls Gap Road and likely at Euroa.The Seven Creeks at Euroa is currently at 2.29 m and steady, below the minor flood level. 

The Seven Creeks at Euroa is likely to exceed the minor flood level (2.50 m) overnight Tuesday into Wednesday. The creek may peak above the minor flood level (2.50 m) Wednesday morning.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground. 
  • Download your Local Flood Guide to understand local flood impacts. www.ses.vic.gov.au/plan-and-stay-safe/flood-guides/strathbogie-shire-council
  • Review your emergency plan and check your emergency kit is fully stocked, if you have one. 
  • Monitor weather forecasts and river levels. Go to www.bom.gov.au/vic/warnings/
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
  • Heavy rain over fire affected areas increases the risk of landslides. 
  • At the minor flood level of 2.5 metres on the Seven Creeks at Euroa, the Euroa Caravan Park, Rotary Park, and park area around the Memorial Oval and Bowls Club may start to be inundated with water.  
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 01/07/2026 03:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.webBody:
This Flood Watch is being issued for the Buckland, Buffalo and Ovens River catchments including 15 Mile Creek.
  • This Advice replaces the Advice issued at 7:48pm Tuesday 30 June.
  • A series of cold fronts are forecast to cross Victoria through to Friday, which is likely to bring widespread moderate rainfall across north east and central Victoria from Tuesday.
  • This rainfall is likely to result in minor to moderate flooding from Tuesday across the Flood Watch area, with isolated major flooding possible.
  • Flood Warnings are current in nearby catchments, including the King River and Seven Creeks.
  • Catchments in the Flood Watch area are relatively wet. The situation is being closely monitored, and Flood Warnings will be issued as required.

properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the Kiewa River downstream of Mongans Bridge.

Renewed minor flooding likely at Kiewa Mainstream and Bandiana from Tuesday evening.

Moderate to heavy rainfall is forecast for the remainder of Tuesday and is expected to continue through to Friday, which is likely to result in renewed river level rises across the Kiewa River catchment. Renewed minor flooding is likely at Bandiana from Tuesday evening and along the Kiewa River mainstream from Wednesday morning. A Flood Watch is current for Parts of North East Victoria.

Kiewa River downstream of Mongans Bridge:
Minor flooding is likely along the Kiewa River at Kiewa Mainstream and Bandiana.

The Kiewa River Channel at Kiewa is currently at 2.38 m and steady, below the minor flood level. The Kiewa River Channel at Kiewa is likely to reach around the minor flood level (3.30 m) Wednesday morning. Further rises are possible with forecast rain. The Kiewa River at Bandiana is currently at 2.15 m and steady, below the minor flood level. 

The Kiewa River at Bandiana is likely to reach around the minor flood level (2.80 m) Tuesday evening. Further rises are possible with forecast rain.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Contact family members and neighbours to ensure they are aware of the situation.
